epigraph.text: A cord of three strands is not quickly broken.

content.text:
We don't need to verify all six parts match to prove triangles 
congruent. Certain combinations of sides and angles are sufficient.

content.title: SSS (Side-Side-Side) Congruence
content.content:
If three sides of one triangle are congruent to three sides of 
another triangle, then the triangles are congruent.
content.label: Postulate

content.title: SAS (Side-Angle-Side) Congruence
content.content:
If two sides and the **included angle** of one triangle are 
congruent to two sides and the included angle of another triangle, 
then the triangles are congruent.
content.label: Postulate

content.text:
The angle must be **included**—that is, formed by the two 
sides. SSA (Side-Side-Angle) does NOT prove congruence!

content.title: Using SAS
content.problem:
Given: $\overline{PQ} \cong \overline{XY}$, $\angle Q \cong \angle Y$, 
$\overline{QR} \cong \overline{YZ}$

Prove: $\triangle PQR \cong \triangle XYZ$
content.solution:
proof

1. $\overline{PQ} \cong \overline{XY}$ (Given)

2. $\angle Q \cong \angle Y$ (Given)

3. $\overline{QR} \cong \overline{YZ}$ (Given)

4. $\angle Q$ is included between $\overline{PQ}$ and 
 $\overline{QR}$ (Definition of included angle)

5. $\triangle PQR \cong \triangle XYZ$ (SAS Postulate)
proof

content.title: Proof with shared side
content.solution:
proof

1. $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{CD}$ (Given)

2. $\overline{AD} \cong \overline{CB}$ (Given)

3. $\overline{BD} \cong \overline{DB}$ (Reflexive Property)

4. $\triangle ABD \cong \triangle CDB$ (SSS Postulate)
proof

content.content:
Order matters in the congruence statement! Match corresponding 
vertices based on the given congruences.

content.text: Why SSA Is Not a Valid Congruence Criterion

Two different triangles can have two pairs of congruent sides and 
a pair of congruent non-included angles. This is called the 
**ambiguous case**.

marginNotes.text:
The Lord is my rock, my fortress and 
my deliverer.

Do your best to present yourself 
to God as one approved, a worker who does not need to be ashamed.

marginNotes.author: Euclid
marginNotes.text:
Things which coincide with one another are 
equal to one another.
